Defines file templates for different project types in the Pebble firmware SDK.

# Purpose
The JSON configuration file defines a mapping structure for file paths in a software project. It organizes the project into different components such as `rocky`, `app`, and `lib`, each with its own set of default and specific file path mappings. For example, in the `rocky` component, the file `src/pkjs/index.js` is mapped to `rocky/app.js`. The `app` component includes different configurations like `default`, `worker`, `simple`, and `javascript`, each specifying how source files should be mapped to their respective target paths. Similarly, the `lib` component contains mappings for C source files, header files, and JavaScript files. This structure helps in managing and organizing the file paths for different parts of the project, facilitating easier builds and deployments.
